trade.summary: Taiwan is a significant trade partner for Indonesia. Indonesia exports coal, nickel, palm oil, and minerals. Taiwan exports electronics, semiconductors, machinery, and petrochemicals. Taiwan's New Southbound Policy has deepened economic ties with Indonesia. Taiwan is a major buyer of Indonesian nickel for stainless steel production.

diplomatic.summary: Indonesia follows a one-China policy and does not have formal diplomatic relations with Taiwan. Relations are conducted through trade offices (TETO in Jakarta, KDEI in Taipei). Despite this constraint, economic ties are substantial and the New Southbound Policy has deepened engagement. Indonesian workers in Taiwan (400,000+) represent a significant people-to-people link.

military.summary: No official military ties. Indonesia adheres to one-China policy, precluding formal defence cooperation with Taiwan.

financial.summary: Taiwanese investment in Indonesia is significant, particularly in manufacturing, electronics assembly, and petrochemicals. Taiwan has invested in Indonesian nickel downstream processing as part of EV battery supply chains. Many Taiwanese companies operate in Indonesian industrial estates.

energy.summary: Indonesia is a major coal supplier to Taiwan's power sector. LNG trade is also developing. Taiwan has explored investing in Indonesian geothermal energy.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation under Taiwan's New Southbound Policy. Taiwanese universities have scholarship programmes for Indonesian students. Cooperation in agricultural technology, semiconductors, and digital economy is developing.
